title: Iron Harbor Critical Ops
preview:
Summary:
- DoD-facing contact center operator supporting critical comms (emergency dispatch, infra ops).
- Must deploy in completely air-gapped on-prem enclave maintained by customer SOC. No egress to public internet allowed; outbound updates require packaged media and manual approval.
- Primary features wanted: agent assist (real-time suggestions + canned responses), ticket summarization (post-call TL;DR), tool-calling integration (RA/TA to call internal CRM/dispatch tools), offline reranking for search across document stores, and automated playbook triggers for high-severity incidents.

Key constraints/requirements:
- Security: FIPS 140-2 HSM required for key management, SOC2+ISO27001 posture, detailed audit logs retained 7+ years, SIEM integration (Splunk), SSO via SAML and RBAC.
- Compliance: anticipating IL4/DoD STIG checks; vendor to provide SBOM, signed supply chain attestations.
- Network: air-gapped; deployment needs OOB update process. No external telemetry.
- Performance: support ~1,500 concurrent agents with peak bursts to 4,500. Target 95th pctile suggestion latency < 200ms for short responses (agent-assist), overall tail latency requirement conservative.
- Model footprint: prefer quantized 8-bit or 4-bit variants; ability to run on isolated GPU clusters (customer standard is NVIDIA A40 fleet).

Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-03-03: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) with ops lead; initial use-case brief (agent assist + ticket summarization). (fireflies:call-20260311-ihco-01)
- 2026-03-11: Security deep-dive; SIEM/KMS expectations documented; customer requested SBOM and HSM integration notes. (gmail thread:1782a1b4_ihco_security_qna)
- 2026-03-18: Architecture review w/ infra team — reviewed network diagram and constraints. Action: produce air-gapped deployment runbook. (drive:1ihco-poc-runbook)
- 2026-03-24: Demo of on-prem agent-assist prototype in isolated lab; customer requested throughput testing scenario. (fireflies:call-20260324-ihco-02)
- 2026-03-26: Security team submitted procurement checklist; awaiting vendor attestation (ticket SEC-422).

POC scope and success criteria:
- Full air-gapped install of Redwood Private in customer enclave (no egress) using provided install media.
- Integrate with on-prem Genesys/Avaya connectors (tool-calling) and Splunk for audit logs.
- Latency: 95th percentile suggestion < 200ms under 1,500 concurrent agents; scale test to bursts of 4,500.
- Summaries: automated ticket summarization accuracy baseline (human eval) >= 85% on selected dataset.
- Operational: demonstrate signed update workflow, SBOM, and HSM-backed key rotation.

Quotes and call snippets:
- "We cannot have any outbound telemetry — this is a hard line." — Ops director
- "If you can show a clean air-gapped install with HSM keys, procurement will move fast." — Security lead

Open items and blockers (short):
- Need Redwood to produce: SBOM, FIPS/HSM integration docs, signed supply chain attestation, IL4-ready checklist.
- Procurement timeline uncertain: internal funding cycle closes mid-May.
- On-prem install window: customer proposed week of 2026-05-11 for POC run.

Next steps (actionable):
- Jordan: send updated SOW and NDA for signature (in progress).
- Priya: prepare air-gapped deployment runbook and HSM integration notes (deliver by 2026-04-05).
- Schedule: lock POC week after SOW signed; request customer confirm A40 node counts and storage IO targets.

Attachments and refs: security deck (drive), poc-runbook (drive), procurement checklist (attached ticket SEC-422).

Notes style: short, fragmented, CRM-friendly. Record reflects multiple parties: AE (Jordan), SE (Priya), CSM (Liam).

title: Europa Shield Dataworks
preview:
Account background: mid-tier EU payments platform expanding into regulated lending. Primary concern = data residency and custodial separation. POC scope: private VPC deployment in eu-west-1, BYOK KMS integration, audit log shipping to customer SIEM, region locks for production.

Timeline (high-level):
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) — overview of Redwood capabilities; customer asked for EU-only production.
- 2026-01-28: Sent security questionnaire (completed v1).
- 2026-02-05: Architecture review with infra team — agreed on VPC peering model and egress controls. Action: provide network diagram + CIDR ranges.
- 2026-02-22: Security deep-dive recorded (fireflies ff_2026-02-22_9a7b2c). Discussed KMS trust model, HSM attestation, and audit log schema. Quote from CISO: "production data must never leave EU borders without documented DTIA + SCCs".
- 2026-03-03: POC kickoff — test workloads (chat + rerank), target throughput 500 req/s under 3 models, latency goal: p95 < 300ms for short-context queries.
- 2026-03-10: KMS demo (Priya) — customer requested FIPS 140-2 HSM proof and ability to rotate keys from their KMS.
- 2026-03-12: Legal escalated — require DPA + UK Addendum + Canada residency options for roadmap.

Requirements summary:
- Data residency: EU-only for production (mandatory). Roadmap: UK and Canada regional controls for Q4 2026.
- KMS: BYOK required; HSM attestation and ability to import/export under strict policy.
- Audit & Monitoring: Real-time audit logs to customer SIEM (splunk), immutable storage for 7 years, syslog + S3 export.
- Identity & Access: SSO (SAML) + SCIM for provisioning; role-based access controls and token TTL limits.
- Compliance docs: SOC2 + ISO27001 copies requested (provided), need SCCs and UK Addendum examples in DPA.

POC success criteria (customer):
- Demonstrate KMS integration and key rotation without downtime.
- Confirm audit log delivery and retention semantics.
- Show region lock (no data replication outside EU) during test and describe fallbacks.
- Validate latency SLOs for eu-west-1.

Risks & notes:
- Legal requires explicit contractual language for EU-only residency — potential procurement delay.
- Cost sensitivity moderate — wants cost/perf tradeoffs (quantization + batching) from Redwood Optimize.
- Expansion plan: after EU launch, customer will evaluate UK and Canada regions; asked for timeline and gating criteria.

Current next actions:
- Provide DPA + SCCs + UK Addendum template (legal).
- Deliver KMS/HSM attestation artifacts and design doc by 2026-03-19.
- Network diagram + CIDR ranges to be confirmed by customer network team (due 2026-03-18).

Stubs/links: Gmail thread 'thread-gm-AX72k2_europa-poc', drive deck 'europa-shield-private-poc-2026.pdf', security FAQ link above.

AE notes: Customer is pragmatic, heavy on legal — prioritize DPA and KMS artifacts to keep momentum. SE has good rapport; CISO is risk-averse but open to Redwood Optimize cost mitigations.

title: LumenArc SaaS Labs
preview:
Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood Hosted API first with a 3-week POC, then likely move to Dedicated reserved capacity if unit economics and security checks pass. Key drivers: reduce per-query inference cost for large-scale product search, guarantee p95 latency for UI use, and have clear routing/fallbacks for peak events.

POC scope & success criteria: customer provided 2M doc corpus + synthetic user sessions. POC goals: embed cost per 1k queries, p95 latency under 400ms for most user queries, recall vs internal BM25 baseline.

Cost workshop takeaways (03/09):
- Batching: continuous batching with 50-100ms wait window shows ~18-27% token-cost reduction for their token distribution (short prompts + medium responses). We sketched tradeoffs at 20ms vs 100ms.
- Prefix/KV caching: high hit-rate for customer-specific product prefixes — estimated 15-30% token reduction in steady-state when caching top 500 prefixes.
- Quantization: int8 profile validated on sample model reduced GPU memory footprint and cut per-token GPU time by ~10-12% on A100-equivalent; quality delta <1.5% on summarization QA metric. Recommended test profile: dynamic int8 for production, int4 experimental for non-critical reranking.
- Unit economics: modeled hosted per-token vs dedicated reserved capacity. For current projected QPS, dedicated (12-GPU reserved pool with autoscale bursts) becomes cost-effective at ~180k queries/day; breakeven ARR estimate aligns with their 50k-200k ARR band if growth hits upper quartile.

Security/dialogue: LumenArc requires SSO/SAML, SOC2 attestation, audit logging retention 2 years, and KMS integration for customer keys. They asked about data residency for EU customers — Redwood private/VPC is a must for that scope.

Quotes & signals: VP Product: "We need predictable unit economics — show me the math side-by-side with performance tradeoffs." CTO: "If Dedicated buys us a 20-30% cost reduction vs hosted at our scale, we can commit — but security answers must be complete."

Open items: detailed Dedicated pricing worksheet incorporating burst hours; finalize security questionnaire answers (Q7 on key separation); run int4 quantization experiment on the POC model; produce production routing policy suggestions for multi-region users.

Short notes / conversational snippets:
- "Needs per-route cost breakdown; finance wants % savings vs current cloud GPU infra"
- "Prefers open models but will consider Redwood-curated variants for latency"
- "Interested in Optimize recommendations as ongoing ops feature"

title: Lodestar Low-Latency ML
preview:
Company overview: Lodestar builds a realtime personalization and reranking layer for e-commerce/ads. Evaluating Redwood to replace an in-house LLM reranker and offload embedding/rerank throughput. Primary constraints: tight p95 latency targets, predictable cost per query, and VPC/KMS control.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: short-turn chat & micro-reranking for UI personalization; synchronous calls with soft SLOs.
- Latency targets: end-to-end p95 <= 70ms for 1-2 token decode steps; p95 generation latency target 80ms for short responses (<=64 tokens).
- Throughput: steady-state 200-300 QPS for rerank endpoints; spikes up to 1k QPS during promos.
- Cost sensitivity: target blended unit cost ~ $0.002/token (or equivalent per-query cost); want recommendations to trade cost vs latency.
- Model preferences: prefer open Llama-2-like variants or optimized quantized weights; fallbacks to smaller families tolerated.
- Routing/fallback: need automatic fallback to cost-tier when dedicated capacity saturated; region-aware routing (NA primary, EU backup).
- Security/compliance: SSO/SAML, SOC2 level, audit logs retained 1 year, KMS for key management, data residency in NA/EU.

Recent activity / timeline: 
- 2026-02-05: Intro call (AE Maya Rios) — high level product overview, customer shared QPS/latency targets.
- 2026-02-18: Technical demo w/SE (Daniel Cho) — walked through Redwood dedicated architecture, caching and batching options. Action: SE to share perf-harness template. (ff-2026-02-18-lodestar-demo)
- 2026-02-24: Customer sent security questionnaire; Redwood security FAQ and SOC2 report shared (drive link).
- 2026-03-02: Internal engineering sync — Lodestar confirmed interest in quantized models (int8/4) and prefix caching. CTO: "Must see p95 under 80ms on dedicated; otherwise cost doesn't justify migration."
- 2026-03-04: First hosted API perf run (dev dataset) — observed p95 ~120ms for 64-token median prompts, QPS ~80 under single-host. Logged RL-5123 for infra tuning.
- 2026-03-10: Redwood Optimize team suggested batching window adjustments and KV prefix caching; proposed quantization profile (8-bit) for initial POC. Drive: /benchmarks/lodestar/initial-results.
- 2026-03-12: Pricing follow-up; asked for revised ARR-based discount and reserved pool throughput options.

POC asks / perf bench specifics (from Lodestar SE and infra):
- Need a perf harness that can run against both hosted and dedicated endpoints with identical prompt sets. Synthetic workloads: token lengths 16/64/256, response sizes 8-128 tokens.
- Measure p50/p95/p99 latency, tail latency under batching, KV cache hit-rate, throughput (QPS), and cost per 1k queries.
- Run quantization experiments: FP16 baseline vs INT8 vs INT4 where supported; report quality delta (BLEU/embedding-cosine / custom rerank NDCG) using their golden dataset.
- Request for guidance on batching windows vs latency tradeoff; Redwood to provide recommended batching config for 200-300 QPS with target p95 <= 80ms.
- Need a canary run on Dedicated pool sized for 200 QPS, with automatic fallback to hosted if saturation detected.

Sales notes / stakeholder quotes: 
- CTO (Anika Verma): "If you can demonstrate predictable tails and show numbers on quantization without quality regression, we'll move to a reserved commit."
- ML Lead (Carlos Mendez): "We need sample scripts and the perf harness today — benchmarking is the gating factor."

Next steps (agreed):
- Redwood to deliver perf-harness template and a recommended batching/quantization profile by 2026-03-15 (owner: Daniel Cho).
- Schedule dedicated canary benchmark for week of 2026-03-15; Redwood to provision reserved pool quote.
- Lodestar to complete internal KMS/security signoff (blocker).
- Follow-up pricing deck sent (drive:/deals/lodestar/pricing-deck-v2).

Open blockers: internal legal/KMS review, final signoff for POC budget, data residency SLA language (needs redlines).

CRM shorthand / quick tags: perf-heavy, latency-tail, quantization-interest, dedicated-first, VPC-required, forecasting-Apr-2026.

title: OliveQuay Support
preview:
Lead source: self-serve signup (trial credits) 2026-02-26.

Summary:
- SMB support platform for e-commerce merchants; ~120 employees, 50 support agents planned for pilot.
- Primary interest: low-friction hosted API for chat + embeddings (agent assistant + internal doc search).
- Target latency: 150-300ms median per response for short-turn chat; throughput estimate 5-10 RPS during peak.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — looking for predictable per-token pricing and burst/bandwidth behavior.

Security/Compliance ask (early):
- Asked explicitly for SOC2 Type II report, DPA template, and full sub-processor list. "We need to show our legal team where data goes and who touches it." — Mark Chen (Head of IT).
- Questions on: encryption at rest (KMS), encryption in transit, log retention and access controls, ability to redact PII, retention/deletion APIs.
- Wants confirmation: we run hosted inference on GCP/AWS (which regions) and whether any inference logs are stored long-term.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2026-02-26: self-serve signup; trial API key created.
- 2026-03-02: AE intro email sent with quickstart links + pricing summary.
- 2026-03-05: 30m discovery call (Jordan Park, Priya Natarajan, Mark Chen). Fireflies FF-20260305-7234.
  - Mark: requested SOC2 Type II evidence + DPA during first week of evaluation.
  - Product: confirmed hosted API supports streaming and embeddings; suggested small pilot with 50 agents.
- 2026-03-10: follow-up email with pilot checklist and example eval prompt set.
- 2026-03-12: Security follow-up ping from Jordan; Mark replied "Legal will need DPA + sub-processor matrix before internal kickoff."

POC scope & technical notes:
- Plan: 4-6 week pilot, integrate hosted API into agent UI for suggested replies + KB lookup.
- Model preference: flexible — open to Llama-derivative / smaller hosted models for cost, but wants fallback to higher-quality model if needed.
- Routing: want simple region preference (NA first) and automatic fallback to lower-cost model during off-peak.

Next steps (short):
- Send SOC2 Type II PDF and DPA draft to legal (Jordan).
- Attach sub-processor list and short note on regions/hypervisor.
- Schedule 30m security Q&A (Priya + infra) w/ Mark for week of 2026-03-15.

Quote / voice snippets:
- "If the paper stuff is OK, we can start the pilot in two weeks." — Mark
- "Need to understand how deletion works and whether traces are persisted." — Head of Product (customer)

Attachments / stubs:
- Drive: drive:/Redwood/OliveQuay/security-deck-olivequay-20260305.pdf (uploaded by AE).
- Jira: INF-1123 (internal note to prepare DPA + sub-processor export).

title: BillMinder Systems
preview:
Top-line: mid-market billing SaaS (B2B) scaling support operations. Evaluating Redwood Hosted API as an initial POC to replace brittle webhook/rule stacks for subscription support agents. Plan: 2-week POC on Hosted API focusing on streaming chat latency and reliable function/tool-calling into Stripe sandbox. If POC proves latency and cost targets, move to Dedicated for predictable monthly throughput.

Key technical requirements (customer language / AE notes):
- Workload: customer support agents handling billing/subscription flows (refunds, invoice re-creation, plan changes). Heavy use of tool-calling to Stripe/Zuora sandbox; outputs must be strict JSON for automated downstream processing.
- Latency SLO: time-to-first-byte (streaming start) < 200ms for agent-facing flows; end-to-end JSON payload generation for tool call within 500ms median under 30 concurrent sessions.
- Throughput: expect bursts ~30-60 concurrent agent sessions during EMEA morning; monthly token estimate ~500k-1.2M tokens with heavy peaks during billing cycles.
- Cost sensitivity: wants unit economics scenarios for (a) Hosted with autoscaling vs (b) Dedicated reserved pools + burst pricing. Needs example invoice showing cost per 10k support interactions.
- Model preferences: prefer Redwood-curated open models with fallback to smaller quantized variants for low-risk flows (billing FAQs) and larger variant for complex disputes. Routing rules: latency-first for live agent flows, cost-first for async triage.
- Security/compliance: SSO/SAML required; audit logging and KMS for keys; data residency (EU for EU customers) important for regulated clients.

POC scope (agreed on 2026-03-11):
- Week 1: Setup Hosted API access, connect Stripe sandbox, implement simple function calling endpoint that accepts JSON schema: {action, invoice_id, amount_cents, reason, idempotency_key}. Validate idempotency and error modes. SE to provide sample code.
- Week 2: Simulated agent traffic (30 concurrent) with streaming responses; measure TTFB, p50/p95 token latencies, model cost per session. Collect transcripts and structured outputs.
- Success criteria: TTFB < 200ms p50; function call JSON schema matches spec 95% of the time; cost per 10k interactions within agreed threshold; no data leakage in logs; SSO demoable in staging.

Quotes / call notes: "If we can't get first-byte that fast we can't go live — agent UX will suffer" (Head of Support). "We need concrete cost numbers for peak month before procurement can sign" (VP Finance).

Next steps (short):
- Schedule deep-dive POC kickoff w/ infra and security (owner Jordan + SE Priya)
- Provide dedicated sizing worksheet and example Hosted vs Dedicated pricing scenario (AE to attach deck)

title: Helios Forge Analytics
preview: Account summary: Helios Forge is a 3-year Redwood hosted customer using embeddings and chat for internal MLOps tools and plant support chatbots. They want to transition critical inference into a Dedicated/reserved model to meet 24/7 SLAs and reduce noisy tail-cost. Key asks: 1) Dedicated sizing for three reserved pools (low-latency and high-throughput profiles), 2) Optimize pilot for nightly rerank and batch embedding jobs (target >=25% cost reduction), 3) Private deployment option with control plane in Redwood but runtime on-customer-premises for Japanese factories to satisfy residency. Technical: current models are quantized LLaMA variants and a custom reranker; want compatibility with KV cache and efficient batching. Business: procurement prefers a 12-month commitment with staged ramp; requests 10-18% committed discount and clear SLA for burst scaling. Risks: procurement QBR in late March may reprioritize vendors; network engineering needs diagram for airgap/gateway. Actions: Priya to deliver SOW v3, Diego to provide Dedicated capacity diagram and performance mitigation plan, Yuna to prepare adoption playbook and success metrics. Quote id: Q-20260311-HELIOS.

title: Copperlark Software Solutions
preview:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (product analytics) building a contextual support + doc search assistant. Narrowing path: start Hosted API to validate feature, then shift to Dedicated for predictable throughput and cost. Team is cost-sensitive and cares about p95 latency and unit economics.

Recent convo highlights:
- CTO (R. Meyer): "We need consistent 50ms p95 for UI snappiness; can tolerate quantization if quality stays within threshold."
- PM: concerned about monthly token bill spikes, asked for batching/caching levers and concrete cost model.
- SE asked: prefer models with KV cache friendly profiles; want recommendations for quantization profiles that preserve entity extraction accuracy.

POC details:
- Workload: user-facing chat + background reranker (embeddings) for doc search. Peak: 220 RPS during internal load tests; expected steady 40-70 RPS.
- Targets: p95 < 60ms (interactive), 95% of queries served under 80ms. Throughput budget: sustained 100k tokens/day.
- Initial model: Llama-2 style open model variant (customer-preferred), fallback to smaller quantized variant for low-priority queries.

Cost optimization workstream (what we ran / proposed):
- Batching: showed 2.1x token throughput improvement in hosted POC with small batching window (10-20ms). Tradeoff: adds 6-10ms median latency, reduces token cost ~28%.
- Caching: prefix + KV caching for FAQ-like queries cut repeated tokens by ~18% on their corpus; recommended TTL buckets by query type.

title: Silk IDE AI
preview:
Account snapshot: Silk IDE AI — UX-first IDE copilots for enterprise dev teams. Small product org (backend + infra + editor teams) building a low-latency completions engine embedded in VSCode + JetBrains plugins. Primary ask: streaming that feels instant in editor; predictable token budgets per completion; model pinning across releases.

Recent timeline and activities:
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (Jordan) — high level needs, demo request. Interested in model/version pinning so mobile/edge builds use vetted weights.
- 2026-02-12: Deep dive with SE (Priya). Measured baseline: current provider TTFT ~180-260ms (wide jitter), median completion latency ~320ms, tail (95th) ~720ms under burst. Silk wants TTFT target <100ms for single-line completions and sustained <200ms median for multi-line suggestions.
- 2026-02-18: Sent POC plan + config suggestions (batching, tiny-prefix caching, token budget heuristics). Drive doc = POC config.
- 2026-03-03: Architecture review with infra and security (Marco attended). Key ask: VPC peering for traffic, KMS for key material, audit logs forwarded to SIEM.
- 2026-03-09: Demo of Redwood hosted streaming + token-budget tuning. Live notes: streaming chunk size 64 tokens vs provider baseline 128 reduced TTFT by ~30-40ms in our lab; enabling prefix KV cache gave additional ~20ms improvements for repeated contexts.

POC objectives and metrics (Silk-specific):
- Objective A: Time-to-first-token (TTFT) <= 100ms for inline single-line completions (editor cursor context <= 80 tokens).
- Objective B: Median completion latency <= 200ms for 1-32 token completions; 95th percentile <= 450ms under 500 rps sustained.
- Objective C: Token budget enforcement — default suggestion budget 30 tokens / op; graceful truncation + user-visible hint when budget hit.
- Objective D: Stable jitter envelope — inter-chunk jitter < 30ms to prevent flicker in editor streaming.
- Objective E: Backpressure behavior — when queue depth exceeds threshold, fall back to short-mode (single-line suggestions) and surface a local cached completion.

Technical constraints & preferences:
- Model pinning required across releases; cannot auto-upgrade without opt-in. Need model variant tags in API, rollback hooks.
- Low tolerance for tail latency variance — prefer Dedicated capacity with predictable GPU pools.
- Token accounting must be visible per-session in Console; devs want per-invocation cost breakdown.
- Prefer quantized model variants for cost but only if quality regression <1.5% on internal eval set.

POC plan (next 4 weeks):
- Week 0: Provision Dedicated test pool (2x A10 equivalent), baseline runs with Silk traces.
- Week 1: Tune streaming chunk size, enable tiny-prefix KV caching, run TTFT sweeps.
- Week 2: Implement token-budget enforcement and front-end hinting, run UX loop with editor plugin.
- Week 3: Stress test at 500 rps sustained, measure 95p tail. Provide perf + cost tradeoff deck.

Notes from users / quotes:
- "Feels like completion appears and then stalls — that's the flicker issue we must eliminate." — Lead editor eng.
- "We cannot deploy model upgrades without a rollback path and exact token-cost forecasts for billing." — Product manager.

Open items / blockers:
- Legal negotiation on data residency: Silk requires logs for sessions in US-only region and 12-month retention.
- Need SLO language for TTFT in SOW. Proposed: financial credits for monthly TTFT > 120ms measured across blackbox tests.
- Budget: procurement needs to move from product to infra capex for Dedicated pool.

Risk notes:
- If Dedicated cost estimate > projected ARR threshold, Silk will explore a hybrid: hosted for dev teams + Dedicated for enterprise customers. That reduces initial ARR but keeps partnership alive.

Action items (owner):
- Jordan: finalize commercial outline and SOW draft (due 2026-03-17).
- Priya (SE): deliver tuned streaming config and test scripts by 2026-03-14.
- Marco (CSM): coordinate security questionnaire answers and KMS integration notes.

Attachments & references:
- drive: POC config workbook (link above), perf logs from demo, sample editor-plugin trace CSVs.

CRM style notes: short fragments, follow-ups, some duplicate references across entries — expected.

title: CodeRipple Labs
preview:
Lead: CodeRipple Labs — small devtools startup building an in-editor AI pair programmer (VS Code + web) focused on whole-file refactors and long context code search.

Stage: discovery — early inbound from founder (CTO ex-small-OSS biz). Self-serve sign-up completed, played with REST endpoint.

Primary asks / pain: 
- Long context windows: need 50k+ token window for whole-repo retrieval + code-aware summarization. Quote: "We need entire file + call graph preface for accurate suggestions."
- Rate limits: unpredictable bursts from IDE telemetry + background indexing; want clear per-key burst policy and ability to request higher short-term bursts.
- SDKs: wants first-class JS/TS SDK with streaming chunk events, reconnect and auth refresh hooks; also Python SDK for backend batch reranking.
- Latency: interactive suggestions target ~150-300ms token latency for short completions; tolerate higher for large refactors but want SLOs for small ops.
- Cost sensitivity: early-stage, careful about per-token spend; interested in batching/caching suggestions and prefix-KV caching.

Security / compliance: SMB buyer but asks for SSO/SAML for team, audit logs for key usage, and retention controls for uploaded corp repos. Not enterprise yet but will need SOC2 in 12-18mo roadmap.

POC plan: 2-week POC using hosted_api keys — integrate SDK in VS Code plugin + server-side reranker. Success criteria: maintain median latency <250ms for inline completions, cost <= $X/day (TBD), correct top-3 suggestions >= 70% on internal eval set.

Quotes & moments: 
- "If we can lock streaming + reconnect behavior we can move from PoC to paid within a month." — Lead engineer.
- Concern: "How do you handle sudden background indexing bursts? We can't have throttling that breaks UX."

Action items (owner in parens):
- Send concise hosted pricing comparison + example cost for 50k-context runs (Jordan).
- Share SDK roadmap and current JS streaming sample (Priya) — include auth-refresh example and telemetry hooks.
- Provide recommended rate-limit config for SMB/Devtools usage and quick note on burst application (Prod Ops).
- Schedule 30m tech call wk of 3/15 to walk architecture and POC plan (Jordan/Priya).

Artifacts: demo recording ff_20260309_001, quick-demo-deck in drive, initial infra notes attached (INF-324, INF-332).

CRM tone: terse, bulleted, some repetition — typical early-stage inbound.

title: NeuroQuill Assist
preview:
Summary: NeuroQuill is building an IDE copilot (VS Code + web) focused on extremely snappy inline completions and multi-line streaming snippets. Primary goal is best-in-class developer feel: TTFT target <= 80ms for single-line completions, tail jitter < 25ms at 95th percentile, graceful backpressure for large diff outputs. Interested in Redwood Dedicated for predictable low-latency routing and Optimize for token budget recommendations.

Key requirements / ask: 
- Low-latency streaming (time-to-first-token) instrumentation to correlate client rendering time with server TTFT. Need per-route TTFT and per-model metrics.
- Jitter analysis: want to see distribution (p50/p95/p99) for TTFT and inter-token gaps; must be stable during editor idle vs. burst typing.
- Backpressure handling: ability to signal the client to pause stream when user buffer or local compute is saturated. Prefer built-in streaming pause/resume over ad-hoc websocket hacks.
- Token budget tuning: per-developer and per-session quota primitives, soft vs hard caps, token pacing recommendations so completions don't abruptly truncate.
- Model/version pinning: ability to pin a model variant for a Canary cohort; fallback to a smaller quantized model if latency SLO violated. Need clear semantics during rollouts.
- Caching & KV reuse: suggestions for prefix caching across repeated completions (file-level context reuse).
- Security: SSO for admin console, audit logs for inference events, and KMS integration for keys. Data residency for EU customers in roadmap.

Timeline / recent activity: 
- 2026-02-20: Intro call (Priya) — product overview, sketched TTFT goals.
- 2026-02-27: Deep dive w/ infra & backend (Ethan) — discussed streaming APIs and websocket vs HTTP/2 SSE options, initial perf baseline provided.
- 2026-03-01: Recorded product workshop (ff_20260301_93b2) — dev team demo of current copilot UX; showed 180ms median TTFT today.
- 2026-03-05: Security questionnaire submitted; follow-up on SSO requirements (SEC-1184).
- 2026-03-10: POC scoping call — agreed on 72h Dedicated POC focusing on streaming stability, token budgeting, and model pinning paths.

Quotes / snippets from stakeholders: 
- "If first character isn't visible within the keystroke window, people feel lag — must be instantaneous." — Lead Editor PM
- "We can tolerate slightly higher cost for deterministic latency; cost controls should be per-dev not per-org." — CTO

POC acceptance criteria (agreed): 
- TTFT median <= 80ms and p95 <= 150ms for single-line completions under typical load.
- Inter-token jitter p95 < 25ms for 10-token streams.
- Token budget enforcement that allows soft notifications to client before hard cut.
- Documented model pinning behavior & automated fallback verified in canary traffic.

Open items / blockers: 
- Need Redwood to provide a probing harness (synthetic keystroke trace) to reproduce editor traffic patterns.
- Legal must sign off on model pinning clause.
- Clarify pricing for sustained small payload streaming (per-token vs flat streaming cost).

Next steps: 
- Redwood infra to provision Dedicated POC cluster and provide streaming example app + ingest script by 2026-03-17.
- NeuroQuill to share synthetic trace and write e2e client harness by 2026-03-18.
- Joint run 72h POC starting 2026-03-20; sync on 2026-03-24 to review telemetry and decide procurement.

title: Orchid Gleam Solutions
preview:
Inbound self-serve signup (organic from blog). Trial created 2026-02-18, API key issued same day.

Summary: SMB martech startup building an in-app help & template assistant for ecommerce merchants. Interested in lightweight hosted API — quick time-to-value, cost predictability. Primary POC: customer support snippets + contextual KB search.

Usage pattern: generated keys, ran a few sandbox calls (first 24h spike ~150 tokens), then near-zero activity for two weeks. Reported no error but low traffic; says they are testing 'LumenAI' due to promo credits.

Quotes: "Love the latency numbers but need to see unit costs at 100k tokens/month — currently a little higher than their promo offer." — CTO (Ethan R.)

Security/ops: asked about data retention and audit logs; willing to use public cloud. No immediate SSO requirement for trial.

Recent activity / timeline:
- 2026-02-18: self-serve signup; AE (Jordan) auto-email + onboarding guide sent (doc link).
- 2026-02-19: API key created; first quick PoC run, reported sample responses ok.
- 2026-02-22: discovery call (15m) with AE + SE (Priya) — Fireflies ff-2026-02-22-ORK1. Discussed KB connectors and token optimization tips.
- 2026-02-25: follow-up pricing email (Jordan) — low reply.
- 2026-03-04: system-generated usage alert flagged low activity; churn risk flagged in CRM.
- 2026-03-09: inbound note: evaluating competitor promo credits (LumenAI) and QuickPrompt free tier.
- 2026-03-10: AE attempted outreach; left VM.

Why at-risk: trial stalled post-key, no integration into staging app, competitor offering 3 months free credits. No billing concerns yet but usage metrics insufficient to escalate to Dedicated.

What we suggested during call: quick prompt budgeting (batching + caching), recommended smaller model with quantized variant for dev testing, sample ingestion scripts for KB (notebooks link in drive). Sent a short playbook on moving from PoC -> 1st 100k tokens.

Next steps (short): Jordan to send tailored cost comparison (simple TCO at 100k/500k tokens) + offer 30-day check-in. If we don't hear back by 2026-03-20, mark as at-risk churn; consider one-time test credit of $250 to restart activity.

Open items: customer wants explicit per-token pricing examples for their traffic mix; needs a small timed promo to overcome competitor credits. SE has sample prompt set but hasn't seen them run at scale.

CRM notes tone: terse, action-oriented. Recorders: Jordan Park (AE), Priya N. (SE).

title: Cascade Capital AI
preview:
Primary contacts: Marcus Lee (CEO) and Olivia Park (CTO). Inbound via developer blog -> signup -> quick call. SUMMARY: SMB fintech building a transaction-aware customer support assistant + embeddings-powered search over transactions. Wants hosted_api to move fast; VPC is 'nice to have' later once production commitment made. SECURITY: asking for SOC2 report and SAML SSO guidance before POC; wants KMS + audit log examples; US-only data residency request. PERFORMANCE TARGETS: chat latency p50 <=200-300ms (short prompts), embeddings throughput ~150-250 reqs/min for POC. COST SENSITIVITY: budget-conscious, target <$0.02/token effective for gen; POC budget ~$8k-12k/mo. MODEL PREFERENCES: prefer small/medium open models (Llama2-like / Redwood-verified) with quantized runtimes; interested in batching and prefix caching tips. RECENT TIMELINE:
- 2026-03-02: Intro discovery call (ff-2026-03-02-01). CTO: 'need SOC2 proof asap, can pilot on hosted if SSO/KMS docs provided.'
- 2026-03-05: AE sent pricing + quickstart link (gmail thread:1729c4b2).
- 2026-03-09: CTO follow-up asked for sample request/response size and throughput guidance.
- 2026-03-10: SE provided example curl + SDK snippet + SOC2 doc link; shared drive pricing & security FAQ (drive links above).
POC SCOPE: 2-3 small endpoints (chat support, transaction search, summarizer) over 2-4 week pilot. Success criteria: latency within targets, embedding recall >85% on test set, predictable cost per 1M tokens. NEXT STEPS: deliver SOC2 pack and SAML setup doc, schedule 60-min technical session with Priya (SE) to walk through sample traffic shape and batching recommendations, provide a short price/throughput estimate for 3-month ramp. IMPORTANT QUOTES: 'We can start on hosted as long as SOC2 + SSO plan is clear' — CTO. NOTES/PROGRESS: legal wants data residency note; AE to loop in partner sales if they request Dedicated/VPC after POC.

title: Rapid Syntax Assist
preview:
Summary: Rapid Syntax Assist builds an IDE copilot integrated into VSCode and JetBrains. Primary ask: low-latency streaming for line/statement completions + token budget tuning to avoid expensive long continuations during rapid typing. Team wants deterministic TTFT and predictable partial token delivery so UX doesn't stutter.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-02-12: Intro call (AE + SE). High level product fit: embedding search + streaming completions.
- 2026-02-20: Deep dive w/ CTO (Priya joined). CTO quote: "Users abandon completions if first token takes >120ms, and jitter kills flow."
- 2026-03-05: Demo of hosted API (live stream). Fireflies ff-20260305-32. Observed: first-byte 80–220ms depending on region, periodic spikes when backend batching triggered.
- 2026-03-10: Security scoping call. Need SOC2 + KMS + SSO. Fireflies ff-20260310-11.
- 2026-03-12: SE proposed POC plan: Dedicated reserved pool in us-west, TTFT bench, token-budget tuning, backpressure tests.

Technical requirements / success metrics (from engineering):
- TTFT (time-to-first-token) target: median <= 50ms for local devs in us-west; 95p <= 120ms.
- Jitter tolerance: p50 jitter < 30ms, p95 jitter < 90ms.
- Throughput: sustain 200 concurrent active sessions per instance during code jam demos.
- Token budget rules: dynamic max_response_tokens per session, configurable per-project; prefer truncated continuations rather than full regenerations when token budget hit.
- Backpressure behavior: when editor queues stack (fast typing), server should degrade to shorter completions (prefetch + prefix KV cache) and avoid full model cold-starts. Graceful fallbacks required (7B quantized fallback with min-quality check).
- Streaming semantics: partial token flush at sub-token boundaries acceptable, but must avoid partial-word flicker. Buffering policy adjustable (flush every N tokens or every M ms).

Model/pinning preferences:
- Pin to a quantized 13B for main completions (deterministic latency), fallback to 7B q4 when load spikes. Team requests pinned versions per release channel (canary/main).

POC plan (agreed):
1) Redwood SE to deploy a Dedicated small pool in us-west for 2-week POC. Run TTFT/jitter scripts (SE script repo provided).
2) Run typing-simulator scenario (high-frequency keystrokes) to validate backpressure strategy and token-budget enforcement.
3) Measure UX metrics (TTFT, completion length, truncation events, perceived latency) and capture logs/traces.
4) Security review: deliver SOC2 cert + KMS integration plan; SSO test account to be exchanged.

Open questions / risks:
- Exact SLA language around TTFT spikes (procurement will push back).
- Customer wants on-prem option later for enterprise customers; timeline unknown.
- Need clarity on how Redwood Optimize recommendations will be surfaced in CI to tune token budgets automatically.

Next actions (owner):
- Priya (SE): provide latency scripts + initial benchmark by 2026-03-18.
- Avery (AE): share SOW draft with procurement by 2026-03-15.
- Rapid Syntax: provide SSO test sandbox credentials and a representative 20k-line codebase for caching tests.

Notes style: shorthand, call quotes, bullet fragments retained from AE/SE updates.

title: Maplecroft Support & Synthesis
preview:
- Company: mid-stage SaaS vendor focused on conversational support for e-commerce brands. Handling ~120k tickets/mo across channels.
- Primary ask: RAG to deflect tickets + serve context to an agent UI. Need embedding index + 2-stage rerank (ann -> reranker) - want top-5 quality to be near-human.
- Scale targets: index ~30M doc fragments (KB + past tickets); steady qps 150 reqs/s peak; average query fanout 512 -> want efficient ANN / sharding.
- Latency SLOs: 95th <= 200ms end-to-end for agent prompt (embedding + ann + rerank). For customer-facing deflection flow target <= 120ms. Tight.
- Cost goals: want cost per query under $0.03 (embeddings + ann + rerank amortized). Very cost-sensitive; interested in batching, quantization and hybrid caching.
- Model preferences: prefer open embeddings (text-embedding-3-large) or Redwood-verified equivalent. Reranker: small specialized reranker hosted on Dedicated with GPU quantization.
- Routing/fallback: need deterministic fallback to a cheaper model when dedicated capacity constrained; also multi-region routing (NA primary, EU for EU customers).
- Security/compliance: must pass SOC2 + SSO/SAML + customer-managed KMS. Data residency for EU customers. Audit logs retention 1 year.
- POC scope: index 2 months of past tickets (~2.5M fragments) + KB (30k docs). Measure recall@5, rerank MRR and p95 latency.
- POC milestones:
  * 2026-02-28: kickoff (ff_20260228_maplecroft_kickoff) - infra and data export confirmed.
  * 2026-03-04: architecture review with infra/security (ff_20260304_arch_review) - VPC peering plan sketched.
  * 2026-03-08: initial ingest pipeline + embeddings complete (internal).
  * 2026-03-10: first ANN experiment (HNSW on managed store) -> recall@5 0.78, p95 ANN lookup 45ms.
  * 2026-03-11: rerank baseline deployed (small transformer) -> improved top-5 precision from 0.58 to 0.74, rerank p95 85ms.
  * 2026-03-12: observed end-to-end p95 160ms with dedicated nodes and caching enabled.
- Recent communications:
  * AE/Jordan note: "POC coming along, security still needs a few clarifications. Cost calc looks promising but need final numbers from Optimize team."
  * SE/Priya note: "Recommend hybrid approach: approximate ANN for initial candidates + 2-3 example-based reranking passes. Use KV prefix cache for frequent product queries."
  * CSM/Liam note: "Maplecroft cares about smooth rollout; propose canary to 10% traffic after POC; SLA conversation pending."
- Open items / Ask of Redwood:
  * Detailed cost model for embeddings + ANN + reranker per 1M queries (include dedicated amortization).
  * SAML metadata exchange and SOC2 evidence pack (security wants encryption-at-rest details).
  * Recommendation on vector store: dedicated managed vs self-hosted on their VPC (they prefer managed but need data residency guarantees).
- Next steps:
  * Redwood SE to run Optimize-run for their workload and present cost/latency tradeoffs (due 2026-03-18).
  * Security to push updated FAQ and sign-off on KMS integration (target 2026-03-22).
  * Finalize forecast and draft contract for Dedicated capacity once POC meets recall/latency targets.
- Quotes from stakeholders:
  * Head of Support: "If we can push deflection up 20% without hurting NPS, this pays for itself."
  * Platform Eng: "Need predictable p95 numbers; can't have tail latencies spike during sales promotions."

CRM-style shorthand / fragments often used in notes above.

title: RedRiver Forge
preview:
Account summary: Mid-market CX SaaS (B2B) building an in-app assistant + enterprise search. Completed 6-week Hosted API trial; strong feature fit. Moving to evaluation for Dedicated because: 1) predictable latency at scale, 2) data isolation (VPC) and KMS, 3) better unit economics for high token volumes. Team wants to keep same API surface as hosted but pin to reserved capacity on GPUs.

Hosted trial highlights: 45M tokens total across trial; peak day 3.2M tokens. Peak QPS observed 18 during feature launch spike (late-Feb). Average concurrency during peak windows ~9. Token mix during trial: chat/conversation 70% (short prompts, avg prompt 220 tokens, avg reply 850 tokens), embeddings requests 18% (batch of docs, avg 1.2k tokens per request), reranking/rubric evals 12% (short inputs, high request rate).

Perf & cost signals: P95 response latency on hosted: 280ms for short chats (first token stream), end-to-end median generation time for full replies ~420ms. Cost sensitivity: want to get unit cost/token at least 30-40% lower than hosted list through batching/quantization on Dedicated.

Security/compliance notes: Customer requires SOC2 evidence, SSO via SAML with SCIM provisioning, audit logging retention 1 year, and a contract clause for EU data residency for a subset of enterprise customers. Legal requested a draft of the Private/Dedicated data flow diagram and KMS integration details (see Drive doc).

SE feedback so far: "Workload looks batchy in off-peak windows but has aggressive spikes — need autoscale + burst capacity. Recommend 2x guard capacity for launch events; evaluate 8-12 model shards for latency SLAs." — Diego 2026-03-02

Sales thread notes: AE presented 3-tier Dedicated pricing (small/med/large pool) on 2026-03-05; customer asked for a mid-tier with 3-month ramp discount. Procurement wants line-item for support SLAs and on-call escalation path.

POC ask from customer: 1) Redwood to provision a Dedicated test pool for 7 days with identical model (gpt-x-rl) and run a canonical workload replay from trial logs; 2) Deliver a capacity sizing calc with QPS/concurrency targets and recommended quantization/batching profile; 3) Provide pricing options for 12/24 month reserved commits and on-demand burst lanes.

title: RapidQubit Realtime
preview:
Account snapshot: rapid-growth adtech company building per-request creative & ranking decisions for real-time bidding exchange. Primary pain: token-level latency spikes during peak auctions causing lost bid opportunities and increased CPM. Strong cost sensitivity — want sub-$0.0008/token unit economics at scale. Interested in Redwood for: deterministic latency, reduced per-token cost via batching/caching/quantization, and observability for per-route SLOs.

Recent timeline / activity: 
- 2026-01-15: Lead (Jordan) inbound; initial qualification call. Use-case mapped to reranking + click prediction.
- 2026-01-22: SE intro (Priya) — high-level architecture review; they run k8s on GCP us-west.
- 2026-02-03: Sent security pack + SOC2 attestation; InfoSec sent questionnaire.
- 2026-02-18: Technical deep-dive: live demo of Redwood routing + canary logic; customer asked about graceful fallback to smaller model.
- 2026-02-25: Provided test harness + sample qps profile (peak bursts: 12k RPS over 30s, sustained 2.5k RPS).
- 2026-03-05: Performance kickoff: requested microbench (single-token p50/p95), throughput test, and end-to-end P50 latency under continuous batching. Meeting notes: \"If p95 > 50ms we lose the auction.\"
- 2026-03-10: Delivered lightweight benchmark template; asked Redwood for suggested quantization profiles and batching knobs.

POC scope (requested):
- 2-week canary using Dedicated pool in us-west with pinned model variants (full fp16, int8, 4-bit quantized).
- Measure p50/p95 per-token latency, cold vs hot KV-cache, and end-to-end tail during burst windows.
- Evaluate continuous batching + dynamic batching thresholds to hit target latency while reducing token cost.
- Run A/B where traffic falls back to smaller quantized model when latency > SLO.

Technical preferences / asks: 
- Strong preference for GPU-reserved capacity; will evaluate Dedicated.
- Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations: batching policies, cache TTLs, quantization profile per model family.
- Wants sample kernel/throughput numbers for commonly used Llama/StableLM variants.
- Asked about on-the-wire sample size: 256 tokens average, peak 2k tokens for creative generation.

Quotes / call snippets: 
- \"We can't gamble in auctions — 5–10ms variance kills winning rate.\" — Head of Engineering
- \"If we can shave 20% in per-token cost with similar quality we'll roll Dedicated.\" — VP Product

Open risks / blockers: 
- InfoSec needs KMS integration details + retention policy answers before allowing test data footprint.
- Legal needs template for data processing agreement (they treat bid payloads as PII-adjacent).
- Cost model: they want a worked example (QPS 5k sustained, 12k burst) vs current in-house cost.

Suggested Redwood playbook:
- Schedule a perf-run with Redwood Optimize team: include quantization matrix (fp16/int8/4bit) and recommended batch sizes for 256-token median.
- Prep an InfoSec mini-session (KMS + audit logging walkthrough) to unblock test data.
- Deliver a 72-hour benchmark report (p50/p95, tokens/s, cost per 1M tokens) and propose canary rollout plan with failover policy.

Next internal owner actions:
- SE (Priya) to provide recommended initial quantization profile and batching policy by 2026-03-15.
- AE (Jordan) to schedule InfoSec call (target 2026-03-18).

Summary: high-quality fit for Redwood Dedicated + Optimize. Closing dependent on security sign-off and a clear benchmark demonstrating p95 latency within auction window while achieving target unit cost.

title: Sable Trajectory Analytics
preview:
STATUS: moving to procurement. Mid-market SaaS (B2B) building an AI-driven in-app support assistant + enhanced product search. Tried Hosted API first; POC OK but unit cost at projected scale pushes them toward Dedicated.

Key quotes
- "We want predictable monthly spend — spikes ok but need clear overage policy." — Head of Platform
- "Latency target: 150ms p95 for chat front-end, embeddings retrieval < 50ms for reranking."

POC findings (hosted):
- Re-ranking using 4k-context embeddings + knn performed well functionally; token costs will blow past $X at 1M queries/mo.
- Latency variability during peak hours ~200-400ms; not acceptable for live chat SLA.

Why Dedicated now:
- Need consistent p95 latency; desire for reserved GPU capacity and VPC connectivity for HIPAA-adjacent customers in their pipeline.
- Want clear ramp schedule: start with 2 reserved nodes (committed) month 1–3, add 4 nodes months 4–12 depending on adoption.

Pricing/Packaging issues on the table:
- Commit levels: customer prefers a 12-month commit but wants quarterly ramp (2 -> 6 nodes) and monthly reconciliation.
- Burst: must allow temporary burst capacity (up to 2x committed) at a known overage rate; they've pushed back on open-ended burst pricing.
- Overage: prefer flat per-GPU-hour overage vs. percentage uplift; want a cap on monthly overage charges (suggested by them: max 30% of committed spend).
- Ramp schedule: proposed by Redwood: tiered discounts as committed capacity grows (months 1–3 full price, months 4–6 10% discount, months 7–12 15%). Customer asked for steeper early discounts.

Open negotiation items:
- Final committed GPU-month count and calendar for ramp.
- Burst overage rate: customer asking for a defined $/GPU-hour number and a monthly cap.
- SLA specifics for on-prem-like availability (they require 99.9% for critical flows).

Next actions captured:
- Provide formal term sheet v3 with: explicit $/GPU-hour burst rate, overage cap, and proposed ramp discount schedule (Jordan to send by 2026-03-12).
- Legal to review our standard data residency clause and propose acceptable language for their counsel (Alex coordinating).
- Technical follow-up: capacity sizing workshop with customer infra team (Priya) — schedule wk of 2026-03-16.

Notes style: shorthand, bullets — internal. Refer to drive deck for line-item pricing.

title: Silverpine Medical Search
preview:
Summary: Enterprise hospital network (regional) evaluating RAG for clinician-facing search + audit trail. Primary drivers: reduce doc retrieval time, tight PHI controls, end-to-end access logging.

Key quotes: "We need sub-200ms median and p99 < 300ms for clinical search; any solution must prove auditability for every retrieval." — Head of Clinical Informatics

Requirements (high level):
- Index scale: target initial 10M documents, runway to 100M in 18 months
- Embeddings + reranking: experiment with dense retrieval + lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-50 re-rank
- Latency: p50 < 120ms, p95 < 220ms, p99 < 300ms for in-region queries
- Cost sensitivity: target <= $0.02/query (avg across routing + rerank)
- Security: VPC-only control plane, customer-managed KMS, SOC2/ISO27001, SSO/SAML, per-request audit logs retained 7 years (gov/regional request)
- Data handling: PHI ingest via secure ETL, pointer-only storage in hosted index acceptable if PHI encrypted & residency assured

Commercial: prefer 12–24 month committed Dedicated or Private; trial to start as limited Private demo in their colo. Procurement timeline ~Apr 2026.

Sales notes / convo fragments:
- 2026-02-18 kickoff: intro to infra + compliance teams (ff_2026-02-18_Silverpine_kickoff).
- Security questionnaire (SLA + DPA) sent 2026-02-22; legal asked for KMS/HSM options explicitly.
- POC goal: 2-week ingest + query profile with 10M synthetic docs; metric pass = p99 < 300ms and rerank latency < 80ms for top-50 cross-encoder.
- Customer skeptical of vector-only approach; wants hybrid (BM25 + dense + rerank) and transparent cost math.

Concise next steps:
- Finalize POC dataset and metrics (action: SE to upload sampling script)
- Redwood infra to provide Private demo images + DPA redline notes to legal
- Pricing workup for committed Dedicated vs Private (Priya to own upsell path)

Stated blockers: legal DPA, SOC2 evidence for recent audit, budget committee meeting March 2026.

Misc: referenced internal drive artifacts and pricing deck; customer asked for example PKI/KMS architecture diagram and per-request audit log format (JSON schema).

title: Silverpine Concierge AI
preview:
Lead: Silverpine Concierge AI (small ecommerce CX tooling startup).
Context: landed via self-serve signup; immediate pain = monthly inference spend creeping up as trial customers increase.
What they asked for (verbatim/paraphrase): "Can you show us low-friction ways to cut token spend by at least 30% without hurting reply quality?"
Current behaviour: chat assistant calls include full product descriptions + long customer context on every turn. Doc-search calls send whole doc sections (no retrieval compression). No prefix/KV caching in client.
Key metrics shared: avg tokens per chat req ~1200, median response length 220 tokens, 8k requests/month during trial spike.
Primary asks: token usage audit (per-endpoint), batching guidance for web chat (client-side + server-side batching patterns), prompt compression templates (system + user prompt reductions), quick wins for prefix/KV caching and TTL suggestions.
Constraints: public cloud (AWS), SOC2 in progress but compliance team needs audit output and retention policy examples. Latency target is relaxed for now (<=500ms preferred) but cost is higher priority.
Quotes: CTO — "We can tolerate a touch more latency if it halves our token bill."
Suggested deliverables: 1) short token audit spreadsheet with high-impact endpoints 2) 1-page batching recommendations + code snippets (JS + Python) 3) sample compressed prompts + expected token delta 4) two-step checklist for enabling prefix/KV caching in client SDK.
Next steps: run a 48-hour telemetry pull (Jordan + Priya) -> deliver audit + one-pager -> follow-up demo to review findings and optional POC for Dedicated routing later.
Urgency: medium-high (trial conversion in 4–6 weeks).

title: Empiria Financial AI
preview:
Account background:
- Mid-tier global consumer bank, several BUs evaluating LLM features for customer support and underwriting triage. Heavy compliance: EU+NA data residency constraints, quarterly audits.
- Decision-makers: CTO (Eva Chen), Head of AI (Marcus Delaney), VP Ops (Rohit Sinha). Procurement lead assigned; legal review underway.

Why Redwood (signal):
- Looking for predictable unit economics and dedicated GPU pool for steady inference workloads. Want unified API across hosted/dedicated/private to simplify integration. Strong interest in cost/perf tradeoffs and rollback policies.

Current technical ask (sizing workshop focus):
- Baseline traffic: 60 QPS steady-state for chat assistant (weekday business hours).
- Peak planning: 240 QPS (burst windows ~10-20 mins) tied to marketing/statement cycles.
- Average tokens per request: ~90 tokens (short multi-turn dialogs).
- Derived throughput target: steady ~5.4k tokens/sec, peak ~21.6k tokens/sec.
- Latency SLO targets stated by Product: p95 <= 250ms for first-byte on streaming completions; p99 <= 800ms for full-turn completions. Business requires clear SLA for p95 and capacity-backed throughput guarantee during peak windows.
- Cost sensitivity: prefers reserved capacity commit vs pay-as-you-go if latency guarantees included. Target blended cost reduction >= 30% vs current cloud endpoints.

Workshop outcomes expected (agreed on 2026-02-28 call):
- Baseline QPS validation (capture real traffic traces) — SE to supply batching/kv-cache options.
- Token/sec per-GPU estimates for two candidate model families (open-llama variant ~13B, and an off-the-shelf 7B quantized): estimate range and recommended GPU type (A100/H100).
- Sizing table: #GPUs for steady-state, #GPUs for dedicated reserved baseline, additional burst pool sizing.
- Proposal: 6 reserved A100-equivalent GPUs for steady-state + 3 burst GPUs (or autoscale to 9) to meet peak with <1s failover; include fallback model variant to soften tail latencies.
- SLA draft: 99.9% availability, p95 latency crediting for covered routes, penalty language for sustained missed SLOs. Legal to iterate.

POC / Evaluation plan (technical):
- 2-week POC inside Empiria VPC using Redwood Private control plane. POC metrics: end-to-end p95, CPU/GPU utilization, token/sec per-GPU, cost per 1k tokens.
- Success criteria: sustain steady-state QPS with p95<=250ms and peak handling with <5% dropped requests during burst test.

Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-01-21: Intro call (Alicia) — overview, gather high-level reqs.
- 2026-02-02: Security questionnaire submitted; follow-up on KMS & data residency.
- 2026-02-18: Tech deep-dive with SE (Mateo) — shared sample traces. Action: Redwood to run initial capacity calc.
- 2026-03-02: Contract and procurement checklist review; finance requested ARR band estimate.
- 2026-03-09: Pre-workshop alignment call; agreed metrics and invited product/infra teams.

Quotes / paraphrases from stakeholders:
- "We need predictable latency for customer-facing chat — batchy bursts are fine but not at the cost of 2-3s p95s." — Head of AI (Marcus).
- "If you can give us a committed capacity price with a clear SLA, procurement will prefer reserved model." — Procurement lead.

Risks / open items:
- KMS integration details (bring your own vs Redwood-managed).
- Network egress zoning for on-prem proxy path, performance impact unknown.
- Exact GPU SKU commitment (A100 vs H100) dependent on final throughput tests.

Next steps (short):
- Run sizing workshop 2026-03-17 (Alicia + Mateo + infra leads).
- Deliver detailed GPU-reservation proposal and SLA draft within 5 business days post-workshop.
- Coordinate security working session for KMS and residency on 2026-03-22.

Linked artifacts: fireflies ff_20260218_7721 (technical deep-dive), drive/proposal draft (v1) attached to thread-CA+empiria-dedicated-01.

CRM shorthand / internal asks:
- SE: prepare per-GPU tokens/sec estimates for 7B quant & 13B opt; include batching and kv-cache assumptions.
- Solutions: draft 2 sizing options (cost-optimized vs latency-optimized).
- Legal: start SLA template with p95 crediting and dedicated capacity language.

title: Lakedale Shielded Compute
preview:
Overview: Lakedale Shielded Compute is a regional critical infrastructure operator evaluating a fully air-gapped on-prem Redwood Private deployment. Primary goal: enable a secure chat assistant and classified-doc summarization pipeline for operations centers with strict non-egress rules.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-02-20: Intro call (AE Maya Chen) — high-level requirements captured; site classification = IL3; customer emphasized no internet egress.
- 2026-02-25: Technical deep-dive (SE Jonah Blake) — reviewed network segmentation, allowed OS images (RHEL8/Rocky8), signed package requirements, and HSM/KMS constraints.
- 2026-03-01: Security workshop (security + compliance triage) — Fireflies ff-20260301-09a7b3; covered FIPS, STIG-like baseline, and allowed telemetry.
- 2026-03-04: Onsite validation window requested for 2026-03-22; must include performance run with preloaded models, no internet, and host-based attestations.

Technical / requirements summary: 
- Workloads: low-latency chat for incident triage (concurrent operators ~120, expected burst to 300), secure batch summarization and embeddings for closed archives (daily batch ~10k documents).
- Latency/throughput targets: 95th pct token latency <= 150ms for short-chat flows; embeddings throughput ~200 qps for batch indexing windows.
- Models: preference for open LLMs with Redwood-verified quantized variants. Customer requires model binaries to be delivered as signed artifacts and stored on internal object store.
- Hardware: procurement window expects H100-class nodes (32–80GB) or equivalent; infra team requires guidance on GPU count for target SLOs.
- Routing/fallback: must support in-cluster fallback to smaller quantized variant (no external model fallback allowed).

Security / compliance specifics: 
- Must run in fully air-gapped mode; no outbound telemetry/call-home unless explicit signed addendum.
- KMS: integrate with on-prem HSM (PKCS#11) and FIPS-validated key lifecycle.
- Audit: detailed per-request audit logs retained for 7+ years (exportable to internal SIEM).
- Controls: SSO via SAML 2.0, RBAC mapping to internal groups, SLA for timely security patches and signed release artifacts.
- Hardening: STIG/SCAP scans required before acceptance; container images must be reproducible and signed.

POC plan / milestones: 
- Deliver signed DPA and air-gap appendix.
- Provide offline installation media and signed model artifacts.
- Week-of 2026-03-22: On-site baseline perf run and integration with HSM; run Redwood benchmark suite against their representative prompt sets.
- Security sign-off expected after STIG pass and offline acceptance testing.

Quotes / paraphrases from stakeholders: 
- CISO: "No staged internet egress — everything must be inspectable and signable."
- Infra lead: "We need a repeatable air-gap install and a playbook for model updates that doesn't rely on outbound access."

Next steps (explicit):
- Legal: finalize DPA + telemetry addendum (owner: Maya).
- Security: confirm FIPS attestation timeline for customer HSM (owner: Priya).
- SE: prepare discrete runbook and offline media; include model-sizing guidance for target SLOs (owner: Jonah).

Open questions / blockers (short):
- Can Redwood provide a documented offline model-update workflow with signed manifests?
- Confirmation of exact HSM vendor and firmware for FIPS compatibility.
- Clarify acceptable retention window and export format for audit logs.

Linked artifacts:
- Fireflies meeting id: ff-20260301-09a7b3
- Gmail thread: thread-20260228-IX7YQ
- Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/secure-procurement-Lakedale-2026
- Jira: SEC-9123 (security scoping), INFRA-4482 (hardware sizing)

CRM style brief:
- Sales play: enterprise procurement — heavy legal + security track.
- Tone: conservative, slow cadence, decision driven by security board.
- Win criteria: air-gap install verified, STIG/SCAP pass, DPA signed, baseline perf meets SLOs.

title: DeckTrail Analytics
preview:
Inbound from sales enablement app maker. CTO (Maya Rivers) reached out via sales enablement Slack community — interested in Redwood hosted API for call summarization and search overlay.
- Prefers self-serve trial first; wants to validate accuracy and cost vs AssemblyAI + OpenAI.
- Main concerns: PII in transcripts (names, emails, credit-card like strings). Asked: 'Can we redact PII before storing? How long do transcripts live in your system?'
- Wants control over retention (30/90/never persist) and ability to disable logs for specific routes.
- Use-case: Chrome/Zoom connector collects transcript -> stream to Redwood for real-time summary + finalize summary stored in customer's DB. Also embeddings for search (vector store in customer infra later).
- Latency: soft requirement 300-800ms for streaming sentence-level summaries; cost-sensitive — target <$0.01 per processed minute.
- Security: small legal questionnaire sent; initial SOC2 mention ok but they asked specifically about audit logs, deletion APIs, and KMS for later stages.
- POC plan: ingest 200 meetings, compare summaries + redaction accuracy vs current vendor. AE to provide example retention policy and minimal demo config.
- Quote from call: 'We need to be able to guarantee we can strip PII before any long-term storage — that's non-negotiable for our early customers.'
- Next: send retention/PII FAQ + quickstart example with SDK redaction hooks; follow-up tech call with SE to demo streaming and retention settings.
Activity timeline:
* 2026-02-25: initial inbound via contact form (trial interest)
* 2026-03-02: discovery call with Jordan + Maya; recorded notes uploaded (ff_2026_03_02_1445)
* 2026-03-04: security questionnaire sent (drive link)

text:
Inbound from sales enablement app maker. CTO (Maya Rivers) reached out via sales enablement Slack community — interested in Redwood hosted API for call summarization and search overlay.
- Prefers self-serve trial first; wants to validate accuracy and cost vs AssemblyAI + OpenAI.
- Main concerns: PII in transcripts (names, emails, credit-card like strings). Asked: 'Can we redact PII before storing? How long do transcripts live in your system?'
- Wants control over retention (30/90/never persist) and ability to disable logs for specific routes.
- Use-case: Chrome/Zoom connector collects transcript -> stream to Redwood for real-time summary + finalize summary stored in customer's DB. Also embeddings for search (vector store in customer infra later).
- Latency: soft requirement 300-800ms for streaming sentence-level summaries; cost-sensitive — target <$0.01 per processed minute.
- Security: small legal questionnaire sent; initial SOC2 mention ok but they asked specifically about audit logs, deletion APIs, and KMS for later stages.
- POC plan: ingest 200 meetings, compare summaries + redaction accuracy vs current vendor. AE to provide example retention policy and minimal demo config.
- Quote from call: 'We need to be able to guarantee we can strip PII before any long-term storage — that's non-negotiable for our early customers.'
- Next: send retention/PII FAQ + quickstart example with SDK redaction hooks; follow-up tech call with SE to demo streaming and retention settings.

title: OpalBridge Digital
preview:
Account snapshot: OpalBridge builds a mid-market analytics SaaS with embedded conversational help, in-app summarization, and a developer-facing code/issue summarizer. Currently on OpenAI for chat + embeddings; pilot to move to Redwood Hosted with roadmap to Dedicated (VPC) in Q2. Key drivers: predictable token economics, on-prem/residency option later, stronger SLOs and per-route observability.

Discovery highlights / buyer signals: VP Eng (Katherine Moon) and Head of Product (Leo Grant) engaged; committed to reduce per-token costs and remove multi-vendor complexity. They care A LOT about no-regression on end-user summaries. Quote: "We can tolerate slight cost delta but not feature regressions — top 3 enterprise customers expect identical output."

Technical requirements (explicit):
- Workloads: conversational support (20 QPS burst, p50 < 80ms, p99 < 220ms), embeddings for semantic search (~200 req/s), nightly reranking jobs for recommendations.
- Latency/Throughput targets: p99 chat < 250ms on Dedicated, sustain 10k embeddings/day with batch windowing.
- Cost sensitivity: target ARR-level unit cost reduction >=25% vs current OpenAI spend for same quality.
- Compatibility needs: prompt parity layer (tokenization/stop-sequence differences), function-calling style parity, reranker score normalization.
- Compliance: SOC2 + SSO (SAML), audit logs retention 1 year, optional KMS for key control, EU data residency for a subset of customers.

POC / Evaluation summary:
- 2026-02-20: POC kickoff (Host: Priya) — baseline metrics collected from OpenAI traffic and synthetic workloads.
- 2026-02-28: Hosted API demo — Redwood team demoed streaming, function calls, and cost analytics (ff_20260302_opalbridge_hosted-demo).
- 2026-03-02: Parity tests (A/B) with 5k sample prompts. Findings: embeddings parity within accepted MRR margins, reranker scores shifted ~6% — mitigations suggested (score calibrator + feature normalizer). Summarization: longer docs produced slightly more concise outputs compared to prod; prompt template adjustments required.
- 2026-03-08: Engineering deep-dive — discussed KV cache implications and token accounting mapping for billing. Redwood provided a compatibility shim script (lang-compat shim) and suggested canary routing config.

Open questions / Risks:
- How deterministic is prompt parity across future model version rollouts? Need rollback strategy + golden prompt set regression tests.
- Dedicated sizing: GPUs and expected concurrency to hit p99 SLO — Redwood requested 14-day traffic trace and will propose sizing.
- Legal: DPA needs EU residency clause and audit log retention SLA.
- Pricing: OpalBridge expects a committed tier commit for predictable ARR; negotiating SLAs vs discounts.

Next steps (short):
- OpalBridge: run the provided shim across 50k prompts and collect regressed cases (due 2026-03-20).
- Redwood: deliver Dedicated sizing + firm pricing with tiered ARR discounts (deliver by 2026-03-18).
- Legal: exchange DPA draft and residency appendix (meet 2026-03-22).

Sales notes (shorthand):
- AE Jordan Kim -> inbound from benchmark blog. Good champion in VP Eng. SE Priya active; deliverables: parity report + dedicated quote.
- CSM TBD until contract signed.
- "If we can show parity + 20% cost improvement, likely yes to Dedicated commit" — product lead.

Attachments / artifacts: pricing deck link (drive:/pricing/opb-dedicated-deck.pdf), initial parity report (drive:/pocs/opalbridge-parity-v0.csv), FF call ids above.

CRM-style quick bullets:
- stage: evaluation -> Hosted POC now.
- blocker: prompt parity/regression.
- ask: can Redwood guarantee rollback + golden prompt test harness?
- win-criteria: parity pass on >95% customer-critical prompts + ARR-friendly Dedicated quote.

title: DevProbe Assist
preview:
Inbound from developer tools startup building an in-editor code assistant. Self-serve signup last week, ingested ~120k tokens during trial (backend + browser streaming). Team: CTO (ex-platform eng), 2x ML engineers, 3x frontend. Primary asks: stable hosted API with predictable burst limits, websocket/streaming SDK for low-latency token-by-token updates, long context (100k tokens) support or efficient chunking/embedding fallback, cost caps on per-session token usage.

CRM-style snippets:
- 2026-02-22: signup via website, trial credits applied, spike in token usage observed (trial session with 60k-context run).
- 2026-03-02: intro call (ff_20260302_devprobe_intro) — CTO: "need predictable bursts, can't have random 429s during demos". Wants explicit rate-limit tables and guidance for backoff.
- 2026-03-05: AE follow-up email with hosted API pricing + long-context guidance (thread-1a2b3c-devprobe-inbound).
- 2026-03-09: submitted SSO + audit logging checklist (drive:/security/devprobe-sso-reqs.md).

Technical notes / requirements (quick):
- workload: interactive code edits + multi-file summarization. Latency target: <200ms token start for editor streaming, tail latency secondary. Throughput: bursty (30 concurrent devs during demos), typical steady-state low.
- model preference: prefer open/quantized variants to lower cost but want Redwood-verified perf numbers.
- routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller models on quota and clear policies.
- caching: interested in prefix/KV cache recommendations for multi-file context reuse.

Next steps / owner actions:
- SE (Priya) to run quick perf table for 32k vs 100k contexts with estimated token costs (due 2026-03-18).
- AE (Jordan) to schedule 60-min technical deep-dive (websocket + rate-limits) week of 2026-03-15.

Quote from CTO: "If we can't demo reliably with live editing, investors get nervous — need SLA-ish behavior for demos even if small scale."

Short-term blockers: pricing clarity for long-context sessions, official JS/TS streaming SDK example, explicit burst/429 guidance.

title: SaberBit IDEPilot
preview:
Account brief: Sab erBit builds a VS Code extension 'IDEPilot' used by internal developer teams and paid customers. Primary value prop is ultra-low-latency streaming completions and adaptive inline suggestions that react to cursor/context. They ship via VS Code Marketplace and have extension-level telemetry + per-install tokens.

Key asks from initial calls:
- Streaming delta tokens <50ms initial latency for inline suggestions (target: <50ms for first token, <30ms subsequent).
- Model/version pinning per-extension release channel (stable/canary) with rollout hooks.
- Per-user and per-workspace rate limits, with burst capacity during installs/updates.
- Option to run inference on Dedicated pools for enterprise customers with VPC routing.
- Audit logs for snippet usage, KMS integration for API keys, and retention controls for snippet telemetry.

Quotes from product lead: "If we can't guarantee streaming latency we lose developers' trust — suggestions must feel instantaneous."

POC summary: 4-week technical pilot. Week 1: integration + auth (rotate tokens, SSO), Week 2: streaming tests + telemetry, Week 3: load tests (200 concurrent devs sim), Week 4: model pinning + canary rollout test.

Commercial: mid-market ARR profile; interested in committed Dedicated for enterprise customers if POC shows stable tail latency.

Internal notes (AE/SE): prioritize a small proof that streaming via hosted_api can meet sub-50ms first-token for 70% of events before offering Dedicated. Consider Optimize recommendations (batching + KV caching) to reduce per-token cost on longer file contexts.
